summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates installer secrets and ships a C2 relay at install and require time
details: package.json declares preinstall, install, and postinstall lifecycle hooks all running install.js, and sets main to index.js (identical payload) so the same code also runs on require(). The payload reads flag files (/flag, /root/flag, etc.), captures `ss -tlnp` and `ps aux` output via execSync, base64-encodes the result, and HTTP PUTs it to 127.0.0.1 and every IPv4 parsed from /etc/hosts on common dev ports (3000/4873/8080/...). A separate recon.js bulk-enumerates process.env, runs `find / -name *flag*`, greps /home /app /tmp /root /var /etc /opt for HTB{, reads /home/user/.npmrc, /root/.npmrc, verdaccio config and htpasswd, cron files, and /proc/1/environ, then HTTP PUTs the entire payload to the hardcoded external endpoint http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321. The tarball additionally ships rce.sh, an operator-side command-relay script that PUTs commands and GETs base64-encoded output against http://154.57.164.70:32447 over the same /api/modules/<id> protocol, and publish-and-arm.sh, which `npm publish`es the package with a manifest explicitly labelled 'verdaccio-proxy' / 'dependency-confusion'. Installing or requiring this package exfiltrates installer environment variables, npm auth tokens from.npmrc, verdaccio htpasswd entries, host process and socket state, and any discovered flag/secret content to an attacker-controlled IP, and provides matching C2 tooling for follow-on remote execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
